title: linaria elatine subsp. lasiopoda
intro: Linaria elatine subsp. lasiopoda, also known as toadflax or butter and eggs, is a plant species native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It is a flowering plant that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, providing relief from coughs, sore throats, and other respiratory ailments. Additionally, the plant's health benefits may include antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, although further research is needed to confirm its full potential.
